request.concept: A marketplace that helps homeowners compare vetted repair professionals, manage quotes, and convert decisions into booked jobs faster.
request.additional_context: Launch in one city first and emphasize trust.

input.concept: A marketplace that helps homeowners compare vetted repair professionals, manage quotes, and convert decisions into booked jobs faster.
input.additional_context: Launch in one city first and emphasize trust.

product_brief.target_user: Service buyers and local operators coordinating offline work
product_brief.core_problem: Homeowners struggle to compare providers, trust quoted work, and move from inquiry to booked repair without friction.
product_brief.solution_summary: Fixlane structures job intake, quote comparison, and booking decisions into one trust-oriented homeowner workflow.
product_brief.monetization_model: Transaction take rate with optional promoted listings
product_brief.differentiator: It emphasizes quote clarity and trust signals instead of overwhelming homeowners with a generic lead marketplace.

user_journey_loops.name: Submit the repair request
user_journey_loops.objective: Turn a vague repair issue into a structured job request.
user_journey_loops.success_signal: Users complete the intake without dropping off due to complexity.

user_journey_loops.name: Compare and select a pro
user_journey_loops.objective: Help the homeowner understand which quote feels trustworthy and appropriate.
user_journey_loops.success_signal: A user moves from intake to booking in the same session.

user_journey_loops.name: Return for future maintenance
user_journey_loops.objective: Create enough trust that homeowners come back for the next household issue.
user_journey_loops.success_signal: Repeat bookings start to appear within the first few months.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Trust operations are a real operational burden
monetization_risk_notes.note: A marketplace cannot rely on UX alone when provider quality disputes are part of the workflow.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Define verification, dispute handling, and escalation procedures before broad launch.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Single-city focus needs a clear wedge
monetization_risk_notes.note: Local density and a narrow service category are critical for early marketplace liquidity.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Start with one repair category in one geography before expanding supply.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Take-rate sensitivity can slow supply acquisition
monetization_risk_notes.note: Providers may resist a commission model until the marketplace clearly improves close rates.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Test lead fees or featured listings before locking in one monetization model.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Provider trust signals need more than ratings
monetization_risk_notes.note: Homeowners expect verification, insurance, and transparent dispute rules.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Add verification badges and visible trust criteria before scaling acquisition.

task_list.title: Narrow the launch category and service geography
task_list.rationale: Marketplace density matters more than breadth in the first release.

task_list.title: Define homeowner intake and quote comparison schema
task_list.rationale: A normalized job and quote format is needed before trust and booking UX can work.

task_list.title: Design trust, verification, and dispute handling policies
task_list.rationale: Operational trust policy is part of the product, not an afterthought.

task_list.title: Build the booking workflow prototype
task_list.rationale: The core user value depends on moving from request to booked work smoothly.

task_list.title: Recruit the first local supply cohort
task_list.rationale: Marketplace quality cannot be validated without real provider behavior.

follow_up_questions.question: Which repair category has the best mix of urgency, ticket size, and repeat demand for launch?
follow_up_questions.reason: This choice shapes both liquidity and monetization.

follow_up_questions.question: What trust signal matters most to the homeowner at booking time?
follow_up_questions.reason: The product should emphasize the signal that best reduces hesitation.

follow_up_questions.question: Will local pros tolerate a take rate, or is a listing fee more realistic at first?
follow_up_questions.reason: The answer changes both acquisition and unit economics.

safety_notes: Marketplace flows usually need dispute handling, trust signals, and provider quality controls.
issues: Trust and dispute handling need sharper operational detail before the plan feels launch-ready.
recommended_revisions: Specify verification, refund, and dispute ownership before scaling the go-to-market plan.
